text: The United States Senate passed a sweeping climate bill authorizing federal investment in renewable energy infrastructure and carbon capture technology across several states.
The House of Representatives debated an appropriations bill funding border security and immigration enforcement, with amendments proposed by both parties during the markup session.
A bipartisan resolution urged ratification of the nuclear arms reduction treaty, citing national security interests and the need for renewed diplomatic engagement with allied nations.
Lawmakers introduced legislation imposing trade sanctions and export controls on strategic semiconductor technology to protect domestic manufacturing and supply chain resilience.
The judiciary committee held confirmation hearings for a federal appellate judge, questioning the nominee on constitutional interpretation, precedent, and administrative law.
A new energy policy framework proposed tax incentives for solar and wind power generation while phasing out subsidies for fossil fuel extraction on public lands.
